## Section 1 — Module Identity

### Bounded Context

{One to two sentences. What domain does this module own? Use domain language, not technical language.}

**Example:** "The Auth module owns user identity verification and session token lifecycle. It is the single authority on whether a given credential is valid."

### What This Module Does NOT Do

{Explicit exclusions. This is as important as what the module does — it prevents scope creep and coupling.}

- Does not manage user profiles or preferences (→ User module)
- Does not control access permissions or roles (→ Permissions module)
- Does not send emails or notifications (→ Notifications module)

## Section 2 — API Contract (Functions This Module Exports)

> For Standard API Modules: these function signatures are the contract. They drive both the TypeScript exports and the generated REST endpoints. Every function listed here must be implemented and exported.
>
> For Web UI / Mobile App: replace this section with "Pages / Screens" and the API modules consumed.
> For UI Component Library: replace with component specifications and prop interfaces.

### Function: {functionName}

**Purpose:** {one sentence}

**Signature:**
```typescript
// Note: only use synchronous signatures when there is zero I/O (e.g. JWT decode + expiry check).
// If the function touches a DB, file, network, or external service — it must be async.
export async function {functionName}(
  {paramName}: {Type},
  {paramName}: {Type}
): Promise<{ReturnType}>
```

**Parameters:**
| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| {paramName} | {Type} | Yes/No | {description} |

**Returns:** `Promise<{ReturnType}>`
- `{field}`: `{type}` — {description}
- `{field}`: `{type}` — {description}

**Errors:**
| Error | When |
|-------|------|
| `{ErrorType}` | {condition that triggers this error} |

**Generated REST Endpoint:**
```
POST /api/{module-name}/{functionName}
Body:    { {param}: {type} }
Response: { {field}: {type} }
Status codes: 200 (success), 400 (validation error), 401 (auth error), 500 (internal)
```

## Section 5 — Deployment Abstraction

> Read `milo/knowledge/deployment-abstraction.md` for the full pattern and code examples.

**Module type:** Standard API Module ← applies to all three layers below
*(For Web UI / Mobile App: "Consumer only — no generation required")*
*(For UI Component Library: "Component exports only — no REST API")*

### Layer 1: Function Exports

File: `modules/{module-name}/index.ts`

All functions from Section 2 must be exported from this file. This is the primary interface.

```typescript
// modules/{module-name}/index.ts
export async function {functionName}({params}): Promise<{ReturnType}> {
  // business logic implementation
}
```

### Layer 2: REST API Server

File: `modules/{module-name}/server.ts`

Every exported function gets a corresponding POST endpoint. Handlers call the functions directly — no duplicate logic.

```typescript
// modules/{module-name}/server.ts
app.post('/api/{module-name}/{functionName}', async (req, res) => {
  const result = await {functionName}(req.body.{param});
  res.json(result);
});
```

### Layer 3: Smart Call Wrapper

File: `modules/{module-name}/proxy.ts`

Any module that depends on this module imports from the proxy, not from index directly. The proxy checks `DEPLOYMENT_MODE` and routes accordingly.

```typescript
// modules/{module-name}/proxy.ts
import * as module from './index';

const proxy = DEPLOYMENT_MODE === 'distributed'
  ? createRestProxy('{module-name}')
  : module;

export const { {functionName}, ... } = proxy;
```

---

## Section 6 — Success Criteria

The module is done when **all** of the following are true:

- [ ] All functions in Section 2 are implemented and exported from `index.ts`
- [ ] Every exported function has a corresponding REST endpoint in `server.ts`
- [ ] The smart call wrapper in `proxy.ts` correctly routes based on `DEPLOYMENT_MODE`
- [ ] All packages used are from the standards list in Section 4
- [ ] Unit tests exist for all exported functions (happy path + each error case)
- [ ] Integration tests exist for all REST endpoints
- [ ] `global-picture/api-docs/{module-name}.md` is created with final function signatures
- [ ] No functions are exported that are not listed in Section 2 (no scope creep)
- [ ] Any function that validates a token or signature does so cryptographically — not just by decoding the payload
- [ ] {feature-specific criterion}
- [ ] {feature-specific criterion}
